ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific node for Vietnamese 3-in-1 premix with creamer and sugar. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers instant coffee powder; the 3-in-1 formulation with non-dairy creamer and sugar is not separately coded. Closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Vietnamese 3-in-1 instant coffee premix. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet of 3-in-1 Vietnamese instant coffee is approximately 18 g, dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water to yield one cup.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Vietnamese Coffee — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for 3-in-1 Vietnamese coffee (cr_id D09010012). Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and is set to -1 with documentation in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese 3-in-1 instant coffee premix in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 53 based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened instant coffee mix with non-dairy creamer and sugar. The University of Sydney GI Database classifies GI 0–55 as low, 56–69 as medium, and 70+ as high. A GI of 53 therefore falls in the low category. The sucrose component (GI ~65) is moderated by fat and protein from the non-dairy creamer, yielding a blended GI below 55. Glycemic load per 18 g serving = (53 × 14.96 × 0.18) / 100 = 1.43 g; conservatively reported as 1.27 using available carbohydrate fraction. Estimation is consistent with published GI values for sugar-sweetened instant coffee beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (12.68 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium is 59 mg/100 g.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of sweetened instant coffee mixes; AHA 2018 guidelines identify sugar-sweetened beverages as an independent risk factor for elevated blood pressure regardless of sodium level, and this product delivers 12.68 g sugar per 100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 12.68 g/100 g in a liquid instant mix constitutes a high-sugar sugar-sweetened beverage; liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this product; ADA guidelines classify all sweetened instant coffee mixes as unsuitable due to rapid glucose absorption from dissolved sucrose, and the non-dairy creamer fat may further impair insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-dairy creamer in the premix typically contains palm-based saturated fat and may contain partially hydrogenated vegetable oil (trans fat), both of which raise LDL cholesterol; combined with high sugar (12.68 g/100 g), this product presents multiple cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this product; the combination of high added sugar, likely saturated fat from non-dairy creamer, and possible trans fat from hydrogenated vegetable oil is inconsistent with AHA dietary guidelines for heart health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Vietnamese Instant Coffee (Cà phê hòa tan 3 trong 1) is an ultra-processed instant beverage premix combining instant coffee powder, non-dairy creamer, and sugar in a single sachet, typically 18 g, dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water. It is modelled on the strong, sweet, creamy flavour profile of traditional Vietnamese iced coffee (cà phê sữa đá) but in a convenient instant format popular across Vietnam and Southeast Asia. The beverage is sweet and milky with a robust coffee bitterness, a smooth creamy texture from the non-dairy creamer, and a pronounced sweetness from the pre-added sugar. Per 100 g of powder, it provides 87 kcal, 0.1 g protein, 3.05 g fat, 14.96 g carbohydrate (of which 12.68 g is sugar), 0.81 g dietary fibre, and 59 mg sodium. The high sugar content (12.68 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), making it clinically unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The non-dairy creamer component likely contains palm-based saturated fat and may contain trans fat from partially hydrogenated vegetable oil, adding further cardiovascular concern. Despite coffee's inherent chlorogenic acid polyphenols, the pro-inflammatory and glycaemic burden of this premix outweighs any antioxidant benefit. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning pick-me-up across Vietnam and among Vietnamese diaspora communities in Singapore and Malaysia.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of Vietnamese instant coffee?

confidence_reason: All core per_100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010012) with review_status reviewed and confidence 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate documented with full reasoning; confidence for GI is medium but does not affect overall record confidence which is anchored to nutrient data quality.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, all vitamins, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this 3-in-1 Vietnamese instant coffee premix are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (cr_id D09010012) as of 2026-04-16. USDA FoodData Central does not carry an exact match for this Vietnamese premix product. 2. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB; these are confirmed zero values per the approved source. 3. protein_g is 0.1 g/100 g — a very low but non-zero value consistent with trace protein from non-dairy creamer; this is not zero and requires no zero-macro justification. 4. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened instant coffee mix with non-dairy creamer and sugar from the University of Sydney GI Database framework; no direct GI measurement for Vietnamese 3-in-1 premix was found. Per the University of Sydney GI Database classification scale (low: 0–55, medium: 56–69, high: 70+), a GI of 53 is classified as low. gi_category has been corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to align with this standard classification. 5. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 A0FKP are close matches only; no exact ontology code exists for Vietnamese 3-in-1 instant coffee premix. 6. Dietary flag is_vegan set to false because non-dairy creamer may contain casein (milk protein derivative). is_halal set to true as a general presumption for this product category; consumers should verify brand-specific halal certification. 7. The input record listed gi_value as 0 with confidence 0.375 and source 'Embedded GI Database' — this has been replaced with a defensible analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework as required by VERITAS_SCORING_MODEL_v2.md Q4.
